title: Getting started with treasury
text: interest rate functions share date argument: pass year yyyy, month yyyymm, leave NULL download full history. daily par yield curve commonly used: remaining interest rate functions follow pattern: call returns long-format table date column, label column (maturity type), corresponding rate/value.
code: yield_curve = tr_yield_curve(2025) head(yield_curve) #>          date maturity  rate #>        <Date>   <char> <num> #> 1: 2025-01-02  1 month  4.45 #> 2: 2025-01-02  2 month  4.36 #> 3: 2025-01-02  3 month  4.36 #> 4: 2025-01-02  4 month  4.31 #> 5: 2025-01-02  6 month  4.25 #> 6: 2025-01-02   1 year  4.17 tr_bill_rate(2025)        # secondary market treasury bill rates tr_long_term_rate(2025)   # 20- and 30-year long-term rates tr_real_yield_curve(2025) # par real yield curve (TIPS) tr_real_long_term(2025)   # long-term real rate averages

title: Getting started with treasury
text: Treasury also publishes historical coupon-issue corporate bond yield curves Excel files. exposed tr_curve_rate(), tr_par_yield(), tr_forward_rate(). first argument selects curve ("hqm", "tnc", "trc", "tbi"), type selects "monthly" "end--month" data: functions require readxl package (install.packages("readxl")).
code: tr_curve_rate("tbi")                    # breakeven inflation curve breakeven rates tr_par_yield("tnc")                     # TNC par yields, monthly average tr_forward_rate("trc", "end-of-month")  # TRC forward rates, end of month

title: Getting started with treasury
text: every function returns tidy table, results plug directly ggplot2. example, 10-year par yield 2025:
code: library(ggplot2)  subset(yield_curve, maturity == "10 year") |>   ggplot(aes(date, rate)) +   geom_line() +   scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::label_percent(scale = 1L)) +   theme_minimal() +   theme(axis.title = element_blank()) +   labs(title = "US Treasury 10-year par yield, 2025", subtitle = "Source: US Department of the Treasury")

title: Getting started with treasury
text: treasury can cache API responses disk, re-running query instant avoid hammering upstream feed. Caching default; enable : Put line .Rprofile enable caching every session. Cached responses kept one day default; tune options(treasury.cache_max_age = <seconds>). Inspect clear cache :
code: options(treasury.cache = TRUE) tr_cache_dir()    # where responses are stored tr_cache_clear()  # wipe the cache

title: Getting started with treasury
text: function reference lists every available endpoint. help page documents relevant data source links official Treasury page.

what: US Treasury marketable securities and auction results — tr_auctions
title: US Treasury marketable securities and auction results — tr_auctions
text: Retrieve data US Treasury marketable securities TreasuryDirect API. tr_auctions() returns auction results securities already auctioned, tr_announcements() returns securities whose auctions announced yet held, tr_upcoming() returns upcoming auction schedule.

title: US Treasury marketable securities and auction results — tr_auctions
code: tr_auctions(type = NULL, days = NULL)  tr_announcements(type = NULL, days = NULL)  tr_upcoming(type = NULL)

title: US Treasury marketable securities and auction results — tr_auctions
text: https://www.treasurydirect.gov/webapis/webapisecurities/

title: US Treasury marketable securities and auction results — tr_auctions
text: type (NULL | character(1)) Security type filter , one "Bill", "Note", "Bond", "CMB", "TIPS", "FRN". NULL, types returned. Default NULL. days (NULL | integer(1)) Restrict results securities last days days. NULL, recent securities returned. Default NULL.

title: US Treasury marketable securities and auction results — tr_auctions
text: data.table::data.table() containing securities NULL entries found.

title: US Treasury marketable securities and auction results — tr_auctions
text: functions return every field provided API (around 120 columns), names converted snake case, date columns parsed Date numeric columns parsed numeric. Fields apply given security type returned NA. API returns 250 recent matching securities.

title: US Treasury marketable securities and auction results — tr_auctions
Daily treasury bill rates — tr_bill_rate
text: rates daily secondary market quotations recently auctioned Treasury Bills maturity tranche (4-week, 8-week, 13-week, 17-week, 26-week, 52-week) Treasury currently issues new bills.

title: Daily treasury bill rates — tr_bill_rate
Daily treasury bill rates — tr_bill_rate
text: https://home.treasury.gov/treasury-daily-interest-rate-xml-feed

title: Daily treasury bill rates — tr_bill_rate
text: date (NULL | character(1) | numeric(1)) Date format yyyy yyyymm. NULL, data returned. Default NULL.

title: Daily treasury bill rates — tr_bill_rate
text: data.table::data.table() containing rates NULL entries found. table carries updated_at attribute feed's last update time POSIXct, accessible via attr(x, "updated_at").

title: Daily treasury bill rates — tr_bill_rate
text: Market quotations obtained approximately 3:30 PM business day Federal Reserve Bank New York. Bank Discount rate rate bill quoted secondary market based par value, amount discount 360-day year. Coupon Equivalent, also called Bond Equivalent, Investment Yield, bill's yield based purchase price, discount, 365- 366-day year. Coupon Equivalent can used compare yield discount bill yield nominal coupon security pays semiannual interest maturity date.

title: Daily treasury bill rates — tr_bill_rate
text: tr_bill_rates() deprecated removed future version. Please use tr_bill_rate() instead.

title: Daily treasury bill rates — tr_bill_rate
code: # \donttest{ # get data for a single month tr_bill_rate("202201") #>            date   type maturity value #>          <Date> <char>   <char> <num> #>   1: 2022-01-03  close  4 weeks  0.05 #>   2: 2022-01-03  yield  4 weeks  0.05 #>   3: 2022-01-03  close  8 weeks  0.06 #>   4: 2022-01-03  yield  8 weeks  0.06 #>   5: 2022-01-03  close 13 weeks  0.09 #>  ---                                  #> 196: 2022-01-31  yield 13 weeks  0.24 #> 197: 2022-01-31  close 26 weeks  0.49 #> 198: 2022-01-31  yield 26 weeks  0.50 #> 199: 2022-01-31  close 52 weeks  0.76 #> 200: 2022-01-31  yield 52 weeks  0.77 # or for the entire year tr_bill_rate(2022) #>             date   type maturity value #>           <Date> <char>   <char> <num> #>    1: 2022-01-03  close  4 weeks  0.05 #>    2: 2022-01-03  yield  4 weeks  0.05 #>    3: 2022-01-03  close  8 weeks  0.06 #>    4: 2022-01-03  yield  8 weeks  0.06 #>    5: 2022-01-03  close 13 weeks  0.09 #>   ---                                  #> 2586: 2022-12-30  yield 17 weeks  4.70 #> 2587: 2022-12-30  close 26 weeks  4.60 #> 2588: 2022-12-30  yield 26 weeks  4.77 #> 2589: 2022-12-30  close 52 weeks  4.51 #> 2590: 2022-12-30  yield 52 weeks  4.73 # }

what: Get or manage the treasury API cache — tr_cache_dir
title: Get or manage the treasury API cache — tr_cache_dir
text: tr_cache_dir() returns path cached API responses stored. tr_cache_clear() clears cached responses.

title: Get or manage the treasury API cache — tr_cache_dir
Get or manage the treasury API cache — tr_cache_dir
text: tr_cache_dir(): string path cache directory. tr_cache_clear(): return value, called side effects.

title: Get or manage the treasury API cache — tr_cache_dir
text: cache used enabled options(treasury.cache = TRUE). Cached responses stored 1 day default, can customized options(treasury.cache_max_age = seconds).

title: Get or manage the treasury API cache — tr_cache_dir

what: Download Treasury Coupon Issues and Corporate Bond Yield Curves — tr_curve_rate
title: Download Treasury Coupon Issues and Corporate Bond Yield Curves — tr_curve_rate
text: Yield Curve Treasury Nominal Coupon Issues (TNC yield curve) derived Treasury nominal notes bonds. Yield Curve Treasury Real Coupon Issues (TRC yield curve) derived Treasury Inflation-Protected Securities (TIPS). Treasury Breakeven Inflation Curve (TBI curve) derived TNC TRC yield curves combined.

title: Download Treasury Coupon Issues and Corporate Bond Yield Curves — tr_curve_rate
code: tr_curve_rate(   x = c("hqm", "tnc", "trc", "tbi"),   type = c("monthly", "end-of-month"),   year = NULL )  tr_par_yield(x = c("hqm", "tnc", "trc"), type = c("monthly", "end-of-month"))  tr_par_yields(x = c("hqm", "tnc", "trc"), type = c("monthly", "end-of-month"))  tr_forward_rate(   x = c("tnc", "trc", "tbi"),   type = c("monthly", "end-of-month") )

title: Download Treasury Coupon Issues and Corporate Bond Yield Curves — tr_curve_rate
text: https://home.treasury.gov/data/treasury-coupon-issues--corporate-bond-yield-curves

title: Download Treasury Coupon Issues and Corporate Bond Yield Curves — tr_curve_rate
text: x (character(1)) One following options: "hqm": Treasury High Quality Market (HQM) Corporate Bond Yield Curve. "tnc": Treasury Nominal Coupon-Issue (TNC) Yield Curve. "trc": Treasury Real Coupon-Issue (TRC) Yield Curve. "tbi": Treasury Breakeven Inflation (TBI) Curve. type (character(1)) Either "monthly" "end--month". Default "monthly". year (NULL | integer(1)) Year download. Default NULL. NULL, available years downloaded.

title: Download Treasury Coupon Issues and Corporate Bond Yield Curves — tr_curve_rate
text: data.table::data.table() containing treasury rates.

title: Download Treasury Coupon Issues and Corporate Bond Yield Curves — tr_curve_rate
text: tr_par_yields() deprecated removed future version. Please use tr_par_yield() instead.

title: Download Treasury Coupon Issues and Corporate Bond Yield Curves — tr_curve_rate
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # TBI Treasury Curve Breakeven Rates tr_curve_rate("tbi") tr_curve_rate("trc", "end-of-month", 2024L) # TRC Treasury Yield Curve Par Yields, Monthly Average tr_par_yield("trc") # TNC Treasury Yield Curve Forward Rates, End of Month tr_forward_rate("tnc", "end-of-month") } # }

title: Daily treasury long-term rates — tr_long_term_rate
text: Treasury ceased publication 30-year constant maturity series February 18, 2002 resumed series February 9, 2006. estimate 30-year rate time frame, series includes Treasury 20-year Constant Maturity rate "adjustment factor," may added 20-year rate estimate 30-year rate period time Treasury issue 30-year bonds.

title: Daily treasury long-term rates — tr_long_term_rate

title: Daily treasury long-term rates — tr_long_term_rate
text: data.table::data.table() containing rates NULL entries found. table carries updated_at attribute feed's last update time POSIXct, accessible via attr(x, "updated_at").

title: Daily treasury long-term rates — tr_long_term_rate

what: Daily treasury par real yield curve rates — tr_real_yield_curve
title: Daily treasury par real yield curve rates — tr_real_yield_curve
text: par real curve, relates par real yield Treasury Inflation Protected Security (TIPS) time maturity, based closing market bid prices recently auctioned TIPS --counter market. par real yields derived input market prices, indicative quotations obtained Federal Reserve Bank New York approximately 3:30 PM business day. Treasury began publishing series January 2, 2004. time Treasury released 1 year historical data.

what: treasury: Client for US Treasury XML Feed and Published Data — treasury-package
title: treasury: Client for US Treasury XML Feed and Published Data — treasury-package
text: Download daily interest rates US Treasury XML feed. Leveraging https://home.treasury.gov/treasury-daily-interest-rate-xml-feed, package serves wrapper, facilitating retrieval daily treasury rates across various categories, including par yield curves, treasury bills, long-term rates, real yield curves.

title: treasury: Client for US Treasury XML Feed and Published Data — treasury-package
text: Maintainer: Maximilian Mücke muecke.maximilian@gmail.com (ORCID) Authors: Maximilian Mücke muecke.maximilian@gmail.com (ORCID)

title: treasury (development version)
text: tr_auctions(), tr_announcements(), tr_upcoming() added retrieve US Treasury marketable securities auction results TreasuryDirect API. daily interest rate functions (tr_yield_curve(), tr_bill_rate(), tr_long_term_rate(), tr_real_yield_curve(), tr_real_long_term()) now attach updated_at attribute returned table feed’s last update time. tr_yield_curve() now correctly labels 1.5-month maturity added Treasury feed February 2025 (previously shown 1 _5month).

title: treasury 0.5.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-03-21 Add optional caching API responses via options(treasury.cache = TRUE). Cached responses stored 1 day default can customized options(treasury.cache_max_age = seconds). Use tr_cache_dir() find cache location tr_cache_clear() clear .

title: treasury 0.4.0
text: CRAN release: 2025-08-26 Fixed bug checking {readxl} installation. Improved documentation. tr_bill_rates() tr_bill_rate() tr_par_yields() tr_par_yield()

title: treasury 0.3.0
text: CRAN release: 2025-07-10 Migration data.table package. Internal data manipulation now done using data.table functions return data.table objects.

title: treasury 0.2.0
text: CRAN release: 2024-07-05 Better documentation. Support Treasury High Quality Market (HQM) Corporate Bond Yield Curve data. Support Treasury Nominal Coupon-Issue (TNC) Yield Curve data. Support Treasury Real Coupon-Issue (TRC) Yield Curve data. Support Treasury Breakeven Inflation Curve (TBI curve) data.

title: treasury 0.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2024-03-22 Initial CRAN submission.
